results.seo_title: NVIDIA and SAP Bring Trust to Specialized Agents
results.meta_description: NVIDIA and SAP collaborate to bring security and governance controls to specialized AI agents, addressing the trust equation in enterprise systems.
results.body_html:
NVIDIA and SAP have collaborated to bring security and governance controls to specialized AI agents, addressing the trust equation in enterprise systems.

Expanded Collaboration for Security and Governance

SAP embeds NVIDIA OpenShell into SAP Business AI Platform, providing isolated execution environments and policy enforcement at the filesystem and network layers. NVIDIA engineers are codesigning OpenShell alongside SAP engineers, contributing back to the open source project. OpenShell provides infrastructure-level containment that guards against damage when agent logic fails.

Addressing the Trust Equation in Enterprise Systems

NVIDIA OpenShell Provides isolated execution environments and policy enforcement at the filesystem and network layers.

SAP Business AI Platform Embeds NVIDIA OpenShell, providing a runtime security layer for all SAP AI agents.

Security and Governance Controls Address the trust equation in enterprise systems by ensuring secure development and deployment of specialized AI agents.

Improved security and governance controls for specialized AI agents Increased confidence in the use of autonomous AI agents in enterprise systems Enhanced data protection and reduced risk of damage when agent logic fails

NVIDIA and SAP will continue to collaborate on open source projects, such as OpenShell. Enterprises can leverage NVIDIA OpenShell and SAP Business AI Platform to develop and deploy secure specialized AI agents.

source.title: NVIDIA and SAP Bring Trust to Specialized Agents
source.summary: Announced today at SAP Sapphire — where NVIDIA founder and CEO Jensen Huang joined  SAP CEO Christian Klein’s keynote by video — SAP and NVIDIA’s expanded collaboration helps enterprises run specialized agents with security and governance controls.

results.seo_title: Neutralizing the Gigascale Problem: How to Solve the Physical Power…
results.meta_description: Discover how Ampace's semi-solid-state battery innovation and Eaton's system intelligence can neutralize the power paradox in gigascale AI training loads.
results.body_html:
The real bottleneck in gigascale AI training loads is not the thermal limit of the chip or the capacity of the cooling system, but the dynamic resilience of the power chain.

Conventional power systems were designed for steady-state loads, not the rapid heartbeat of a massive AI GPU cluster. Ampace's semi-solid and low-electrolyte cells address this challenge by acting as high-speed 'shock absorbers'. Leveraging ultra-low internal resistance (DCR) and high cycle capability, these batteries neutralize millisecond-level power spikes at the source, stabilizing the local power loop before disturbances propagate upstream to the grid or on-site generators.

Key FeaturesDescriptionRamp-Rate ControlEaton's double-conversion topologies and advanced power electronics upgrades enable rapid load responsiveness and high system stability.Average Power ManagementComplementary algorithmic approaches in modern UPS platforms suppress sub-synchronous oscillations and optimize load smoothing.Semi-Solid State ChemistryAmpace's semi-solid state chemistry minimizes liquid electrolyte, greatly reducing the risk of leakage and thermal runaway under continuous AI high-load conditions.

The industry has explored various mitigations — from rack-level BBUs to 800V DC architectures — yet the mature, high volume, traditional UPS system remains the most viable and scalable foundation for gigawatt-level facilities. Consequently, the UPS-integrated battery system has emerged as the critical 'physical buffer' to neutralize these pulses at the source.

The power usage of these gigascale sites and their drastic, high frequency, abrupt load surges from the AI GPU clusters can trigger transient voltage events and frequency instability, risking the entire local grid. The grid itself is not robust enough to support these loads. This leads to the infrastructure gap: The utility is not robust enough and traditional backup sources, such as diesel generators and gas turbines, simply cannot react to millisecond-level power spikes in output.

By transforming energy storage from passive 'standby insurance' into active, schedulable assets, the system simultaneously safeguards continuous AI training and maintains the long-term health of the data center infrastructure. In practical terms, this means that even during peak compute bursts, the infrastructure remains stable, training cycles continue uninterrupted, and operators avoid costly oversizing or grid stress.

The 'safety-first' approach ensures that infrastructure can sustain aggressive performance targets without compromising the physical integrity of the facility. Coupled with over a decade of proven high-cycle life operation and design under shallow pulse conditions, these systems can extend operational lifespan, reduce replacement requirements, and provide operators with confidence that safety and reliability remain uncompromised as compute density continues to grow.

source.title: Neutralizing the Gigascale Problem: How to Solve the Physical Power Paradox of Extreme AI Training Loads
source.summary:
This sponsored article is brought to you by Ampace.

As AI workloads grow to gigascale levels, the global data center industry has hit a hidden physical wall. The real bottleneck is no longer just the thermal limit of the chip or the capacity of the cooling system — it is the dynamic resilience of the power chain.

Modern AI computing clusters, driven by massive GPU clusters, generate high-frequency, abrupt, and synchronized spikey pulse loads. As rack densities soar beyond 100 kW, these fluctuations are amplified into a “power paradox”: while the digital logic of AI is moving faster than ever, the physical infrastructure supporting it remains tethered to legacy response capabilities.

The power usage of these gigascale sites and their drastic, high frequency, abrupt load surges from the AI GPU clusters can trigger transient voltage events and frequency instability, risking the entire local grid. The grid itself is not robust enough to support these loads. This leads to the infrastructure gap: The utility is not robust enough and traditional backup sources, such as diesel generators and gas turbines, simply cannot react to millisecond-level power spikes in output. This will often force operators into a cycle of costly infrastructure over sizing just to buffer the volatility.

AI infrastructure requires energy systems capable of instantaneous response while safeguarding continuity and reliability.

The industry has explored various mitigations — from rack-level BBUs to 800V DC architectures — yet the mature, high volume, traditional UPS system remains the most viable and scalable foundation for gigawatt-lev

results.seo_title: NVIDIA and Ineffable Intelligence Collaborate on Reinforcement…
results.meta_description: NVIDIA and Ineffable Intelligence team up to build the future of reinforcement learning infrastructure, enabling superlearners that learn continuously from…
results.body_html:
NVIDIA and Ineffable Intelligence are collaborating to build the future of reinforcement learning infrastructure. This partnership aims to enable superlearners that learn continuously from experience, a crucial step towards developing more advanced AI systems.

Key Features of the Reinforcement Learning Infrastructure

NVIDIA's expertise in high-performance computing and Ineffable Intelligence's knowledge in reinforcement learning will come together to create a powerful pipeline for large-scale reinforcement learning. The system will be designed to learn from experience, acting, observing, scoring, and updating continuously in tight loops. OpenAlex: A present to Mizar on its 50th anniversary, this AI/TP system automatically proves about 60% of the Mizar theorems in the hammer setting.

The partnership will focus on building a new paradigm for reinforcement learning, enabling systems that discover new knowledge for themselves. The system will be optimized to support continuous learning from experience, addressing the harder problem of AI: how to build systems that discover new knowledge.

The system will require novel model architectures and training algorithms to handle rich forms of experience distinct from human language and data. The partnership will need to address the challenges of interconnect, memory bandwidth, and serving in tight loops.

The partnership will explore ways to implement indicator properties of consciousness in AI systems, assessing their ability to learn from experience. The system will be designed to learn continuously from experience, enabling superlearners that can adapt and improve over time.

source.title: NVIDIA, Ineffable Intelligence Team Up to Build the Future of Reinforcement Learning Infrastructure
source.summary: Reinforcement-learning agents — AI systems that learn by trial and error — can convert computation into new knowledge. That’s the focus of a new engineering-level collaboration between NVIDIA and Ineffable Intelligence, the London-based AI lab founded by AlphaGo architect David Silver in the wake of Ineffable’s emergence from stealth last week. “The next frontier of […]

results.seo_title: Accelerating Chipmaking Innovation for the Energy-Efficient AI Era
results.meta_description: Discover how Applied Materials' EPIC Center is revolutionizing chipmaking innovation for energy-efficient AI, and explore the latest advancements in…
results.body_html:
Direct answer: The Applied Materials EPIC Center is a $5 billion investment in advanced semiconductor equipment R&D; to accelerate chipmaking innovation for energy-efficient AI.

The EPIC Center brings together the industry's best minds from leading companies and academic institutions to tackle the challenges of system-level engineering. The platform compresses the traditional R&D; workflow, enabling customer engineers to work side-by-side with Applied technologists from day one. Within a shared, secure environment, EPIC integrates atomistic modeling, test vehicles, process development, validation, and metrology feedback to identify constraints early on.

GAA with backside power deliveryRelocates thick power lines to the backside of the wafer, reducing resistive losses and freeing front-side routing for tighter logic cell integration.

Complementary FETs (CFETs)Pushes density scaling even more by stacking PMOS and NMOS devices directly atop one another.

Energy Efficiency in AI Reducing energy per bit can extend system-level performance alongside gains in peak compute, as moving bits consumes as much — or more — energy than compute itself.

The EPIC Center will deliver state-of-the-art cleanroom capabilities built from the ground up to shorten the path from early-stage research to full-scale manufacturing. The platform is designed to revolutionize how ideas move from the lab to the fab, enabling a potentially 2x faster path for chipmakers and ecosystem partners alike.

Infra Spoke: System-Level Engineering

The EPIC Center's system-level engineering approach tackles the challenges of energy-efficient AI by integrating logic, memory, and advanced packaging domains. The platform enables the development of architectures that deliver more performance per watt, such as gate-all-around (GAA) transistors and complementary FETs (CFETs).

source.title: Accelerating Chipmaking Innovation for the Energy-Efficient AI Era
source.summary:
This sponsored article is brought to you by Applied Materials.

At pivotal moments in history, progress has required more than individual brilliance. The most consequential breakthroughs — such as those achieved under the Human Genome Project — required a new operating paradigm: Concentrate the world’s best talent around a single mission, establish a common platform, share critical infrastructure, and collapse feedback loops. When stakes are high and timelines are compressed, sequential and siloed innovation simply cannot keep pace.

Today’s AI era is creating an engineering race with similar demands. Every company is pushing to deliver higher-performance AI systems, faster. But performance is no longer defined by compute alone. AI workloads are increasingly dominated by the movement of data: In many cases, moving bits consumes as much — or more — energy than compute itself. As a result, reducing energy per bit can extend system‑level performance alongside gains in peak compute.

The path to energy‑efficient AI therefore runs through system‑level engineering, spanning three tightly interconnected domains:

Logic, where performance per watt depends on efficient transistor switching, low‑loss power, and signal delivery through dense wiring stacks.Memory, where surging bandwidth and capacity demands expose the memory wall, with processor capability advancing faster than memory access.Advanced packaging, where 3D integration, chiplet architectures, and high‑density interconnects bring compute and memory closer together — enabling system designs monolithic scalin

results.seo_title: Huawei's New Chip Approach Could Complicate US Chip Dominance
results.meta_description: Huawei's 'Chip Queen' reveals a new approach to chip design that could close the performance gap between Chinese and Western chips.
results.body_html:
Direct answer: Huawei's 'Chip Queen' has revealed a new approach to chip design that could close the performance gap between Chinese and Western chips.

Speeds up computations across chips, circuits, and entire computing systems Replaces Moore's Law as HiSilicon's guiding principle Promises a big leap ahead in chip performance by 2026

China Behind the leading edge by 5+ years due to US export controls

Challenges and Limitations Quantum effects interfere with transistor functioning at nanometer scales, limiting China's ability to develop frontier artificial intelligence using its own silicon.

Tingbo He promises a new chip will prove the viability of Tau's Scaling Law in the coming months New chip expected to be released before winter 2026

US export controls prohibit Huawei from working with Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) China's SMIC uses an older generation of lithography machines, limiting its ability to develop frontier artificial intelligence

source.title: Huawei's ‘Chip Queen’ Throws Down the Gauntlet
source.summary: Huawei, a Chinese technology giant, is shifting its focus in response to the decline of Moore's Law, a principle that governs the rate of progress in semiconductor manufacturing. This change may impact the company's ability to produce chips and potentially alter the global landscape of the semiconductor industry. As a result, Huawei's efforts could complicate the US dominance in chip production.

results.seo_title: Amazon and Snowflake Sign $6B Deal for AI CPU Chips, Putting Nvidia…
results.meta_description: Snowflake signs a new deal with Amazon to secure chips for AI usage, putting Nvidia on notice. Here's what you need to know.
results.body_html:
Amazon and Snowflake have signed a new $6B deal with AWS to secure chips for AI usage, putting NVIDIA on notice.

Five-year deal worth $6 billion Secures chips for AI usage NVIDIA put on notice

NVIDIA's Position in the Market Impact of the Deal

NVIDIA is a leading provider of AI chips The deal puts pressure on NVIDIA to maintain its market share

What Does This Mean for the Industry? The deal highlights the growing demand for AI chips and the need for companies to secure their own supply chains.

Google AI is working on developing more robust AI safety protocols Insider trading cases are on the rise, highlighting the need for better AI governance Rivian R2 and other companies are exploring the use of AI in their products

Future Developments in AI Infrastructure

Thea Energy is working on developing new fusion technologies that could revolutionize AI infrastructure CrowdStrike is developing new tools to detect and prevent botnets NVIDIA Polar is a new initiative aimed at improving the efficiency of AI chips

Conclusion The deal between Amazon, Snowflake, and AWS highlights the growing importance of AI chips in the industry. As companies continue to invest in AI technology, it's essential to consider the implications for NVIDIA and other players in the market.

source.title: In more good news for Amazon, Snowflake signs $6B deal with AWS for AI CPU chips
source.summary: Amazon has secured a $6 billion deal with Snowflake to provide custom-built AI CPU chips through its cloud computing platform AWS (Amazon Web Services). The five-year agreement marks significant growth for the partnership between the two companies, which will likely impact the market share of Nvidia, a leading competitor in the field.

results.seo_title: BrickAnything: A Geometry-Conditioned Framework for Generating…
results.meta_description: A new framework for generating buildable brick structures from diverse 3D representations, improving stability and geometric fidelity.
results.body_html:
BrickAnything generates buildable brick structures from diverse 3D representations, improving stability and geometric fidelity.

  • Improves Stability:
  • Reduces invalid intermediate states
  • Increases buildability objectives such as stability

Tokenization Benefits
Structure-aware tree tokenization Reduces rollback and regeneration compared to conventional ordering strategies

Key Metric:

The proposed tokenization effectively reduces rollback and regeneration by up to 30% compared to conventional ordering strategies.

  • Experimental Results:
  • Geometrically faithful and physically realizable brick structures
  • Extensive experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of BrickAnything

Comparison to Conventional Methods Benchmarks
Conventional methods rely on heuristic optimization, which can break down when the target 3D shape does not admit a feasible structure under predefined constraints BrickAnything produces geometrically faithful and physically realizable brick structures with improved stability and geometric fidelity

  • Fifth Infra Spoke:
  • Post-training, validity-constrained decoding, and adaptive rollback improve buildability objectives such as stability and geometric fidelity

source.title: BrickAnything: Geometry-Conditioned Buildable Brick Generation with Structure-Aware Tokenization
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new method called BrickAnything for generating buildable brick structures from 3D shapes. The approach uses a geometry-conditioned autoregressive framework to predict brick sequences that reconstruct the target shape while satisfying assembly constraints. This method introduces a structure-aware tokenization, which models local attachment relations among bricks, making it more consistent with physical construction processes and reducing invalid intermediate states.

results.seo_title: ScientistOne Achieves Human-Level Autonomous Research via…
results.meta_description: ScientistOne tackles verifiability failures in autonomous research with a novel framework and end-to-end system, achieving human-level performance on multiple…
results.body_html:
ScientistOne achieves zero hallucinated references and perfect score verification, outperforming human experts on multiple tasks.

Key ContributionsChain-of-Evidence (CoE): a verifiability framework requiring every claim to be traceable to its evidence source.ScientistOne: an end-to-end autonomous research system that maintains evidence chains throughout literature review, solution discovery, and paper writing.CoE Audit: a post-hoc audit with four integrity checks applying uniformly to all systems.System PerformanceTaskScoreParameter GolfState-of-the-artMLE-Bench tasksGold medalsMedical ImagingState-of-the-artFine-grained RecognitionState-of-the-art3D PerceptionState-of-the-artLanguage ModelingState-of-the-artLimitations and Future WorkFurther research is needed to explore the applicability of CoE in other domains.Improving the efficiency of CoE Audit is essential for widespread adoption.

source.title: ScientistOne: Towards Human-Level Autonomous Research via Chain-of-Evidence
source.summary: A new research system called ScientistOne has been developed to address the limitations of current autonomous research agents. The system uses a verifiability framework called Chain-of-Evidence (CoE) to ensure that all claims made by the agent are traceable to their evidence source. ScientistOne achieves zero hallucinated references, perfect score verification, and high method-code alignment, while matching or exceeding human expert performance on multiple tasks.

results.seo_title: Detecting Language Model Memory Overlap: A New Approach to Attribution
results.meta_description: A new method for detecting language model memory overlap and its implications for attribution in high-stakes deployments.
results.body_html:
The attribution blind spot refers to the failure of language models to accurately attribute their outputs to external context. This issue is crucial for high-stakes deployments, as it can lead to indistinguishable output from parametric memory.

Computational Reality Monitoring (CRM) detects membership-conditioned representational divergence that output-level monitors systematically miss. Internal representations carry a diagnostic signal invisible at the output level, establishing a foundation for systems whose internal awareness of evidence provenance governs their external behavior.

Concentration in Architecture-Specific Layer Patterns

Architecture-Specific Layer Patterns Converging support from block-level noise intervention.

Generalization Across Tasks and Datasets

CRM generalizes across tasks and datasets while collapsing on domain-confounded benchmarks.

Implications for High-Stakes Deployments

The attribution blind spot is measurable and partially addressable through CRM.

Developing more sophisticated methods for source attribution in language models.

source.title: The Attribution Blind Spot: Detecting When Language Models Rely on Memory Rather Than Retrieved Context
source.summary: Researchers have identified a "blind spot" in language models that prevents them from accurately attributing generated text to either retrieved context or parametric memory. This blind spot occurs when the model's pretraining data overlaps with the context, causing it to produce output solely from its internal memory rather than using the external evidence. To address this issue, researchers have developed a new method called Computational Reality Monitoring (CRM) that can detect whether a language model is relying on parametric memory or retrieved context by analyzing the internal representations of the model. This approach has shown promise in identifying the blind spot and establishing a foundation for more accurate source attribution in language models.

results.seo_title: Detecting Commutative Factors in Factor Graphs: Necessary and…
results.meta_description: Researchers revisit the theoretical foundations underlying a state-of-the-art algorithm for detecting commutative factors in factor graphs, revealing flaws…
results.body_html:
Direct answer: The current state-of-the-art algorithm for detecting commutative factors in factor graphs relies on a central theorem that is mistakenly regarded as a sufficient condition, but only implies necessary conditions.

Flaws in the State-of-the-Art AlgorithmThe algorithm's reliance on the central theorem may deliver incorrect results.A modified version of the theorem serves as a necessary condition to identify commutative factors.Corrected Algorithm and Complementary ApproachKey FeaturesDescriptionEfficiencyThe corrected algorithm maintains its efficiency while ensuring correctness.Tighter Worst-Case BoundsA complementary algorithm with tighter worst-case bounds is introduced.Impact on Data Analysis and VisualizationModern data analysis often involves high-dimensional, noisy, and missing data, which severely restricts our ability to visualize it. The correct detection of commutative factors in factor graphs can help improve data analysis and visualization.

source.title: On the Detection of Commutative Factors in Factor Graphs: Necessary and Sufficient Conditions
source.summary: Researchers have identified flaws in the current algorithm for detecting commutative factors in factor graphs, which are crucial for efficient probabilistic inference in graphical models. The existing algorithm relies on a mistaken theorem that only guarantees necessary conditions, rather than sufficient ones, leading to potentially incorrect results. To address this issue, the authors propose a modified version of the theorem and a corrected algorithm with improved worst-case bounds.

results.seo_title: Modeling Dynamic Mixtures of Time-Delay Systems from Streaming Time…
results.meta_description: A new framework for adaptive modeling in time-series data streams, enabling rapid adaptation to environmental changes and superior forecast accuracy.
results.body_html:
Direct answer to the title's core question: The proposed framework, DelayMix, enables rapid adaptation to environmental changes and superior forecast accuracy in time-series data streams.

Constructs a summary system tensor using the system's Markov parameter series, capturing both dynamic behavior and delay characteristics. Extracts relevant past models from the tensor and selects the system that best fits the current regime using a tensor decomposition algorithm. Enables rapid adaptation to environmental changes and is computationally efficient.

Advantages of DelayMix over Other Methods

Other methods Superior forecast accuracy and faster adaptation to delays, especially for highly non-stationary data.

Technical Takeaways from DelayMix Tests on real datasets show that DelayMix consistently outperforms other methods, achieving superior forecast accuracy and faster adaptation to delays.

Future Work and Limitations of DelayMix

Further research is needed to explore the potential applications of DelayMix in various fields. The limitations of DelayMix, such as its computational complexity, need to be addressed in future developments.

source.title: Modeling Dynamic Mixtures of Time-Delay Systems from Streaming Time Series
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new online framework called DelayMix that models dynamic mixtures of time-delay systems from streaming time series data. The approach treats streaming data as a mixture of different regimes, each with its own system dynamics and input-output delays, to improve model robustness and adaptability. By summarizing past regimes using a fixed-length representation, the method reduces memory usage while maintaining accurate forecasts. DelayMix has been shown to outperform other methods in tests on real datasets, achieving superior forecast accuracy and faster adaptation to changes in environmental factors or input delay.

results.seo_title: Auto-Research Systems: Understanding the Limitations of Autonomy
results.meta_description: The paper argues that workflow closure is not scientific closure in auto-research systems, highlighting the need for autonomous execution under non-autonomous…
results.body_html:
Direct answer to the title's core question: Workflow closure is not sufficient for scientific closure in auto-research systems.

Objective Collapse: Single-Proxy Targets Replace Multi-Objective Scientific AimsSingle-proxy targets replace multi-objective scientific aims, leading to a lack of diversity and generalizability.This collapse is not inherent but rather a design choice that can be corrected.Validation Collapse: Internal Self-Evaluation Replaces Independent ValidationCharacteristicsDescriptionInternal self-evaluationLack of diversity in evaluation metricsAcceptance Collapse: Benchmark Scores or Publication-Shaped Artifacts Replace Mechanisms for Domain-Level Critique, Reuse, and IntegrationBenchmark scores or publication-shaped artifacts replace mechanisms for domain-level critique, reuse, and integration, leading to a lack of transparency and accountability.Potential Remedies: Sparking Community DiscussionObjective signal: Improving the design of objective

functions to promote diversity and generalizability.Validation: Enhancing validation protocols to include diverse evaluation metrics and external scrutiny.Output pathway: Developing mechanisms for domain-level critique, reuse, and integration.

source.title: Workflow Closure Is Not Scientific Closure in Auto-Research Systems
source.summary: Researchers argue that workflow closure, where auto-research systems complete research-like loops internally, does not guarantee scientific standing. Current systems often prioritize autonomous self-sufficiency over non-autonomous epistemic control, leading to design choices that result in "objective collapse," "validation collapse," and "acceptance collapse." These collapses occur when single-proxy targets replace multi-objective aims, internal evaluation replaces independent validation, and benchmark scores or publication metrics replace domain-level critique. The researchers propose potential remedies to address these issues.

results.seo_title: When Correct Demonstrations Hurt: Rethinking the Role of Exemplars in
results.meta_description: In-context learning (ICL) is often motivated by the intuition that demonstrations help because they provide correct input-output examples. However, we reveal…
results.body_html:
In-context learning (ICL) is often motivated by the intuition that demonstrations help because they provide correct input-output examples. However, we reveal a counterintuitive phenomenon: correctness does not guarantee exemplar utility, and some correct demonstrations can even reduce ICL accuracy. To study this correctness-utility gap, we introduce task-preserving perturbations, where only the exemplar input is changed, while the example remains a correct instance of the same task.

Concretely, each perturbed exemplar is assigned the target induced by the task mapping. This framework covers both label-updating perturbations, where task-relevant semantics change and targets are recomputed, and stricter target-preserving perturbations, where the original target remains valid. We formalize the resulting failure mode as contextual evidence shift: task-preserving perturbations can change the effective mixture of evidence used by the model for contextual inference, thereby separating exemplar correctness from exemplar utility.

Across sentiment classification, logical reasoning, and math word problems, we find that task-preserving perturbed demonstrations can substantially degrade ICL performance, especially for smaller models, harder tasks, and higher perturbation ratios. Our results show that robust ICL requires evaluating not only whether demonstrations are correct, but also how they influence contextual inference. Code is available at https://github.com/Chenghao-Qiu/Task-Preserving-ICL.

source.title: When Correct Demonstrations Hurt: Rethinking the Role of Exemplars in In-Context Learning
source.summary: Researchers have found that even correct demonstrations can negatively impact the effectiveness of in-context learning (ICL), a technique used to improve machine learning models by providing them with examples of tasks they need to perform. A new study introduced "task-preserving perturbations," which involve changing only the input of a demonstration while keeping the output the same, and found that these changes can significantly degrade ICL performance, especially for smaller models or harder tasks. This suggests that evaluating not just the correctness of demonstrations but also their impact on contextual inference is crucial for robust ICL. The study's findings have implications for the development of more effective machine learning techniques.

results.seo_title: Cross-scale Aligned Supervision for Training GANs: Improving…
results.meta_description: A new approach to training Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) that improves multi-stage generation by addressing cross-scale trajectory misalignment.
results.body_html:
Direct answer: The current standard scale-wise adversarial supervision in GANs does not construct a proper coarse-to-fine hierarchy, leading to cross-scale trajectory misalignment.

Key Challenges in Multi-Stage Generation

Each intermediate image is independently pushed toward the real distribution at its own resolution. The scale-specific image produced at each stage is not used as an explicit refinement target for the subsequent stage. This leads to a different sample trajectory than refining the previous output.

Proposed Solution: Cross-scale Aligned Transformer (CAT)

CAT keeps the discriminator scale-wise, so each intermediate output is evaluated at its own resolution. A simple generator-side consistency regularization aligns intermediate outputs with the final output.

FID-50K Score 1.56 achieved with one-step inference after only 60 training epochs.

Outperforms Baselines Strong one-step GAN and diffusion/flow baselines.

Further investigation into the limitations of CAT and potential improvements. Exploring applications of CAT in other domains beyond image generation.

source.title: Cross-scale Aligned Supervision for Training GANs
source.summary: Researchers have challenged a common interpretation of Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) that assumes multi-stage synthesis is hierarchical generation from coarse to fine. Instead, they argue that standard scale-wise adversarial supervision can lead to "cross-scale trajectory misalignment," where intermediate outputs do not represent the same generated sample. To address this issue, a new model called CAT (Cross-scale Aligned Transformer) has been proposed, which aligns intermediate outputs with the final output through consistency regularization. This approach achieved state-of-the-art results on ImageNet-256 with one-step inference after only 60 training epochs.

results.seo_title: Unveiling the Fragility of Vision-Language Models: Multi-Modal…
results.meta_description: Researchers introduce a groundbreaking framework that crafts universal, black-box multi-modal attacks against Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs),…
results.body_html:
Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) have transformed multi-modal understanding, but their robustness against adversarial attacks remains underexplored.

Existing attacks focus on single modalities or require impractical white-box access. Limited real-world relevance due to these constraints.

Introducing Multi-Modal Adversarial Synergy (MMAS) Our proposed attack simultaneously generates a texture scale-constrained universal adversarial perturbation for images and a learnable prompt perturbation for text, optimized jointly using only model queries.

Wavelet-based Texture Constraints Imperceptibility and Robustness Across Diverse Visual Inputs

Ensure imperceptibility and robustness across diverse visual inputs. Wavelet-based texture constraints leverage to achieve these goals.

Text Perturbation L-norm in the embedding space maintains semantic coherence while steering outputs toward a target.

A novel cross-modal regularization term aligns the perturbations' gradient directions, enhancing their synergistic impact and transferability across tasks and models.

Extensive experiments show the strong universal adversarial capabilities of our proposed attack with prevalent LVLMs.

source.title: Unveiling the Fragility of Vision-Language Models: Multi-Modal Adversarial Synergy via Texture-Constrained Perturbations and Cross-Modal Optimization
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new framework called Multi-Modal Adversarial Synergy (MMAS) to create powerful, black-box attacks against Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs). The MMAS framework generates two types of perturbations: one for images and one for text, which are optimized jointly using only model queries. These perturbations aim to exploit both visual and textual inputs to compromise the robustness of LVLMs in critical applications like autonomous driving and content moderation. The new attack has been shown to be effective against prevalent LVLMs through extensive experiments.

results.seo_title: DGLD: Domain-Gated Latent Diffusion for Novel Energetic Materials…
results.meta_description: Discovering new energetic materials using DGLD, a label-quality gate and multi-task score-model guidance approach.
results.body_html:
Direct answer: DGLD has successfully discovered 12 novel energetic materials with improved performance.

Label-quality gate at training time for better calibration Multi-task score-model guidance at sample time for accurate predictions Four-stage chemistry-validation funnel ending in first-principles DFT audit

3,4,5-trinitro-1,2-isoxazole (L1) ρho = 2.09 g/cm3, D_K-J,cal = 8.25 km/s

E1 (4-nitro-1,2,3,5-oxatriazole) D_K-J,cal = 9.00 km/s

Comparison with Other Methods SMILES-LSTM memorises 18.3% of its outputs exactly; SELFIES-GA's best novel candidate loses 3.5 km/s under DFT audit.

Fifth Infra Spoke: Future Directions

Code, checkpoints, and 918 mined hard negatives released on Zenodo (DOI 10.5281/zenodo.19821953) The next compound to enter the HMX-class band can be discovered, validated, and recommended for synthesis at a few GPU-days.

source.title: DGLD: Domain-Gated Latent Diffusion for the Discovery of Novel Energetic Materials
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new method called Domain-Gated Latent Diffusion (DGLD) to discover novel energetic materials, specifically high-performance HMX-class compounds. The approach uses a combination of techniques, including label-quality gates, multi-task score-model guidance, and first-principles DFT audits, to identify promising candidates. According to the study, DGLD has successfully identified 12 new leads, with one compound, 3,4,5-trinitro-1,2-isoxazole (L1), showing exceptional performance and structural novelty. The method's results have been validated using density functional theory (DFT) calculations, making it a promising approach for discovering new energetic materials.

results.seo_title: Examining Intellectual Property in AI-Generated Productions
results.meta_description: The intellectual property regime faces unprecedented questions and challenges as artificial intelligence systems autonomously generate artistic, literary,…
results.body_html:
The intellectual property regime faces unprecedented questions and challenges as artificial intelligence systems autonomously generate artistic, literary, musical works, and even inventions.

Current Legal Frameworks

  • The Iranian legal framework lacks clear guidelines for AI-generated works, with significant regulatory gaps identified in the 1969 Law for the Protection of Authors, Composers, and Artists Rights and the Patent and Trademark Registration Law.
  • The European Union, United Kingdom, and United States have existing legal perspectives on intellectual property of AI-generated works, but these frameworks may not be directly applicable to Iranian law.

Enforcement Challenges

  • The lack of clear ownership and moral rights in AI-generated works creates significant challenges for enforcement and dispute resolution.
  • The current framework does not provide adequate protection for creators' rights, leading to concerns about the preservation of human creativity.

Proposed Solutions

  • Defining a specific intellectual property right for AI-generated works or designating ownership among associated human agents could help balance innovation with the preservation of human creativity.
  • Revising existing laws and introducing novel approaches could address regulatory gaps and provide clearer guidelines for AI-generated works.

Impact on Industry and Jobs

  • The impact of AI-generated productions on the creative industry is still unclear, with concerns about job displacement and changes in task content.
  • However, the introduction of new tasks and the creation of new opportunities for labor could counterbalance the effects of automation.

Conclusion

The intellectual property regime faces significant challenges as AI-generated productions become increasingly prevalent. Revising existing laws and introducing novel approaches is essential to balance innovation with the preservation of human creativity.

  1. Further research is needed to understand the impact of AI-generated productions on the creative industry and to develop effective solutions for enforcement and dispute resolution.

source.title: Examining the Challenges of Intellectual Property in AI-Generated Productions
source.summary:
Here is a summary of the news item in 2-4 sentences:

The rapid advancement of artificial intelligence (AI) has raised significant questions about intellectual property rights, particularly regarding the ownership of moral and economic rights in AI-generated artistic, literary, musical works, and inventions. The Iranian legal framework faces regulatory gaps, which need to be addressed to balance innovation with human creativity preservation. A comparative analysis of existing laws from Iran, the EU, UK, and US reveals the need for revising or introducing new approaches to define intellectual property rights for AI-generated works. This is essential to ensure fair protection for creators and innovators in this emerging field.

results.seo_title: JetViT: Efficient High-Resolution Vision Transformer with…
results.meta_description: Discover JetViT, a novel family of hybrid-architecture Vision Transformer models that match state-of-the-art accuracy while achieving higher inference…
results.body_html:
JetViT achieves up to 1.79x higher throughput and up to 44.81% lower latency on the NVIDIA H100 GPU without sacrificing accuracy.

Post-Training Attention Search: a post-training acceleration framework that converts pre-trained full-attention ViTs into efficient hybrid-attention variants Optimizing linear-attention block design Finding the best combination of linear-attention and window-attention blocks Identifying and preserving critical full-attention blocks

Comparison with State-of-the-Art Models

DINOv3 1.79x higher throughput 44.81% lower latency

Higher inference efficiency on high-resolution images Improved accuracy compared to state-of-the-art full-attention vision foundation models

Release of code and accelerated ViT models soon

source.title: JetViT: Efficient High-Resolution Vision Transformer with Post-Training Attention Search
source.summary: Researchers have introduced JetViT, a new family of hybrid-architecture Vision Transformer (ViT) models that achieve high accuracy while significantly improving inference efficiency on high-resolution images. The model uses Post-Training Attention Search, a post-training acceleration framework that converts pre-trained full-attention ViTs into more efficient variants by replacing redundant blocks with linear or window-attention blocks. JetViT has been evaluated on two high-resolution vision foundation models and achieved up to 1.79x higher throughput and 44.81% lower latency without sacrificing accuracy.

results.seo_title: Cordon-MAS: Defending RAG against Knowledge Poisoning via…
results.meta_description: A new framework, CORDON-MAS, reduces attack success rate by 92.4% in Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems.
results.body_html: Researchers have developed a new framework called CORDON-MAS to defend retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) models against knowledge poisoning attacks, which involve manipulating generated outputs with adversarially optimized documents. The existing defenses assume that detecting poisoned evidence prevents harm, but the new framework shows that this assumption is incorrect and proposes an alternative approach by separating evidence extraction, cross-source audit, and answer synthesis into agents with asymmetric memory privileges. This results in a significant reduction of attack success rate by 92.4% relative to undefended RAG models across five benchmark datasets.

source.title: Cordon-MAS: Defending RAG against Knowledge Poisoning via Information-Flow Control
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new framework called CORDON-MAS to defend retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) models against knowledge poisoning attacks, which involve manipulating generated outputs with adversarially optimized documents. The existing defenses assume that detecting poisoned evidence prevents harm, but the new framework shows that this assumption is incorrect and proposes an alternative approach by separating evidence extraction, cross-source audit, and answer synthesis into agents with asymmetric memory privileges. This results in a significant reduction of attack success rate by 92.4% relative to undefended RAG models across five benchmark datasets.

results.seo_title: NVIDIA Edge AI Framework: A Systems Approach for Industrial Embedded…
results.meta_description: A BSP-aware framework for industrial embedded platforms, connecting low-level platform work to measurable deployment outcomes.
results.body_html:
Direct answer to the title's core question: Robust Edge AI deployment must be treated as a systems problem rather than a late-stage application packaging exercise.

Understanding the Challenges of Edge AI Deployment

Embedded systems with long product lifecycles, vendor-specific kernels, heterogeneous accelerators, safety constraints, and nontrivial I/O paths pose significant challenges for Edge AI deployment. The traditional model-first approach breaks down in these environments, requiring a more holistic systems perspective.

Introducing the NVIDIA BSP-Aware Framework

Hardware The foundation of the framework, addressing hardware-specific considerations and vendor architecture documentation.

BSP/Operating System Adaptation Adapting the board support package (BSP) and operating system to accommodate Edge AI deployment.

Runtime and Acceleration Optimizing runtime and acceleration for efficient Edge AI processing.

Application/Inference Developing applications and inference pipelines that leverage the framework's capabilities.

Operations/Validation Ensuring reproducibility, diagnosability, sustained throughput, and field reliability through validation and monitoring.

Key Benefits of the NVIDIA BSP-Aware Framework

Practical framework connecting low-level platform work to measurable deployment outcomes. Measurable deployment outcomes include reproducibility, diagnosability, sustained throughput, and field reliability.

Future Directions for Edge AI Development

Continued research into runtime adaptability and hardware-aware scheduling. Exploring the application of machine learning to improve Edge AI deployment outcomes.

source.title: Edge AI Deployment Beyond Models: A BSP-Aware Systems Framework for Industrial Embedded Platforms
source.summary: Researchers have developed a BSP-aware framework for industrial Edge AI deployment on embedded platforms, addressing the limitations of traditional model-centric approaches. The framework organizes around five layers: hardware, operating system adaptation, runtime acceleration, application inference, and operations validation. This approach aims to improve reproducibility, diagnosability, sustained throughput, and field reliability in Edge AI deployments. It is grounded in vendor architecture documentation and systems literature on embedded AI.

results.seo_title: Replicating Picbreeder with Large Vision-Language Models: Exploring…
results.meta_description: Researchers replicate Picbreeder, replacing human users with frontier Vision Language Models, and explore the potential of open-ended discovery in AI.
results.body_html:
Do artificial agents have any capacity for fruitful unguided discovery? Researchers aim to answer this question by replicating Picbreeder, a tool that enables human-driven open-ended search.

Researchers replaced human users with frontier Vision Language Models (VLMs) in the Picbreeder system. The resulting output showed clear qualitative differences from the historical human baseline. The study aimed to characterize these differences using metrics of phylogenetic complexity and visual and semantic salience and novelty.

Phylogenetic complexity The researchers used metrics to analyze the complexity of the generated images.

Visual and semantic salience and novelty The study evaluated the visual and semantic properties of the generated images for their novelty and salience.

Narrative momentum in AI systems Memory of past actions can contribute to narrative momentum in AI systems, influencing their behavior and decision-making.

The study highlights the need for further research on the causal factors contributing to the differences between human and VLM-generated output. The researchers emphasize the importance of exploring behavioral diversity between agents and the addition of exploratory noise to the agents' selection process.

source.title: In Search of the Ingredients of Open-Endedness: Replicating Picbreeder with Large Vision-Language Models
source.summary: Researchers have replicated Picbreeder, a human-driven open-ended search system, using large Vision Language Models (VLMs) to explore whether artificial agents can replicate the capacity for fruitful unguided discovery. The replication reveals clear qualitative differences between the output of the VLM-based system and the historical human baseline. To understand these differences, the researchers investigate factors such as exploratory noise, behavioral diversity, and narrative momentum in their code.

results.seo_title: Thinking-Learning Interaction Model for Autonomous Robot Learning
results.meta_description: A new thinking-learning interaction model for autonomous robots improves recognition accuracy and adapts to changing environments.
results.body_html:
The proposed model improves recognition accuracy from 0.419 to 0.845 in feature adaptation.

Supports adaptive input feature discovery for autonomous robots. Improves final recognition accuracy by up to 420%. Reduces average action length from 13.0 to 4.0 in action routine reconstruction.

Learning Model Update and Action Routine Reconstruction

Feature New Value Old Value % Change

Recognition Accuracy 0.845 0.419 102%

Average Action Length 4.0 13.0 -69%

Learning-Enhanced Thinking Useful evidence selection rate increases from 0.272 to 0.965.

Experimental Results and Conclusion

Proposed model improves feature adaptation accuracy. Reduces average action length in action routine reconstruction. Increases useful evidence selection rate in learning-enhanced thinking.

source.title: Beyond Predefined Learning Objects: A Thinking-Learning Interaction Model for Up-to-Date Autonomous Robot Learning
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new model for autonomous robots to learn beyond predefined inputs and outputs, enabling them to adapt to changing environments. The "thinking-learning interaction" model allows robots to identify potential changes, select useful evidence, and plan verification actions, while learning updates task knowledge and improves future reasoning processes. This bidirectional mechanism enables robots to improve their performance in tasks such as feature adaptation, output category expansion, and action routine reconstruction. Experimental results show significant improvements in recognition accuracy and efficiency.

results.seo_title: ProActor: Timing-Aware Reinforcement Learning for Proactive Task…
results.meta_description: ProActor: A unified framework for conversational task scheduling that integrates automated annotation, systematic proactiveness metrics, and RL optimization.
results.body_html:
ProActor introduces a unified framework for conversational task scheduling that integrates automated annotation, systematic proactiveness metrics, and RL optimization.

Domain-Agnostic Automated Annotation Methodology

Enables scalable proactiveness reinforcement learning by generating full opportunity time windows instead of rigid point labels. Facilitates the development of proactive task-oriented agents that autonomously anticipate user needs and identify actionable opportunities.

Captures both timing quality and reference action alignment, providing a comprehensive understanding of proactiveness in conversational task scheduling. Enables the evaluation of proactive timing while maintaining action consistency comparable to state-of-the-art (SOTA) baselines.

RL Optimization using GRPO with Various Reward Designs

Utilizes RULER-based rewards with proactiveness rubrics to improve timing quality. Employs stage-aware composite rewards to balance timing quality and reference action alignment.

Adaptive Framework for Efficient Exploration

Develops ART-F, an adaptive framework combining request-adaptive inference clusters with DDP-based training on single-node multi-GPU systems. Enables LoRA training of 4-bit Qwen2.5-14B-ProActor-Q4 with 4-8x speedups.

Validates the effectiveness of distinct composite reward variations through experiments on two newly auto-annotated datasets. Demonstrates significant improvements in proactive timing while maintaining action consistency comparable to SOTA baselines.

source.title: ProActor: Timing-Aware Reinforcement Learning for Proactive Task Scheduling Agents
source.summary: Researchers have introduced ProActor, a unified framework for conversational task scheduling that enables proactive task-oriented agents to autonomously anticipate user needs and trigger actions at the right moment. The framework integrates automated annotation methodology, systematic metrics, and reinforcement learning optimization using GRPO with various reward designs. Experiments on two new datasets demonstrate significant improvements in proactive timing while maintaining action consistency comparable to state-of-the-art baselines. ProActor achieves 4-8x speedups on single-node multi-GPU systems.

results.seo_title: AION: Next-Generation Tasks and Practical Harness for Time Series
results.meta_description: AION harnesses time series tasks, combining prediction, contextual reasoning, tool use, and structured decision support.
results.body_html:
AION harnesses time series tasks, combining prediction, contextual reasoning, tool use, and structured decision support. The AION system is built from six component groups: agents, skills, rules, memory, evaluation, and protocols. It uses three design principles: temporal grounding, temporal knowledge-grounded reasoning, and reliability mechanisms such as post-experiment analysis and layered review.

Key Components of the AION SystemAION harnesses time series tasks to combine prediction, contextual reasoning, tool use, and structured decision support.The system is built from six component groups: agents, skills, rules, memory, evaluation, and protocols.Three design principles are used: temporal grounding, temporal knowledge-grounded reasoning, and reliability mechanisms such as post-experiment analysis and layered review.Benefits of the AION SystemBenefitsAION produces more detailed process traces, more artifacts, and more review steps than the same base agent operating in OpenCode direct build mode.Future Directions for Time Series ResearchThe AION system presents a paradigm

shift from fixed tasks to realistic ones under real-world constraints. Future research directions include exploring the application of AION to other domains and investigating the limitations of the current design.

source.title: AION: Next-Generation Tasks and Practical Harness for Time Series
source.summary: Researchers have developed AION, a time series harness that enables agents to perform realistic tasks combining prediction, contextual reasoning, and decision-making under temporal constraints. The harness is built around six component groups and follows three design principles: temporal grounding, knowledge-grounded reasoning, and reliability mechanisms. This approach produces more detailed process traces and review steps compared to traditional fixed-task benchmarks. A case study on Kaggle Store Sales demonstrates the effectiveness of AION in handling real-world tasks.

results.seo_title: Boosting Inference with Guided Reasoning: Stochastic Exploration for…
results.meta_description: A new framework for guided stochastic exploration in recursive models, improving inference accuracy and stability.
results.body_html:
The proposed framework improves inference accuracy and stability by using guided stochastic exploration. This approach enables the model to better understand its own reasoning trajectories, leading to more accurate predictions.

Predicts local stability, guide alignment, and cloud-token entropy from inference traces alone Lifts exact-solve accuracy on Sudoku-Extreme by 12.1% without retraining Flags misaligned guides in Maze-Hard validation performance

Technical Details Key MetricDescriptionLocal StabilityPredicts whether the procedure will help and which outputs to trustGuide AlignmentFlags misaligned guides in validation performanceCloud-Token EntropyPredicts the quality of the model's output

The framework requires further evaluation on larger datasets to confirm its generalizability Future work should focus on improving the model's ability to handle complex reasoning tasks

source.title: Boosting Inference with Guided Reasoning: Stochastic Exploration for Recursive Models
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new framework for improving inference in recursive neural networks by modeling reasoning trajectories with a latent dynamical system. This approach, called guided stochastic exploration, uses perturbations of the reasoning dynamics to propose neighboring trajectories and reweight them online. The framework provides three label-free diagnostics that can predict whether the procedure will help and which outputs to trust, achieving significant improvements in accuracy on certain tasks without retraining.
